title: VideoBox Properties
description:
VideoBox Properties

VideoBox Properties and Events

The following list is a summary of the VideoBox control properties with brief descriptions.  Click on the property names for more details.

Property	Description

BorderStyle	Gets or sets the control's border style.

Default: 0 - None

Camera	Sets/gets which camera to display video from.

Default: 0

Enabled	Determines if the control is enabled or not.

Default: 1 - True

EventTaskType	The type of task used for events.

Default: 0 - Normal

Font	Selects the font for the text in the control.

Default: Microsoft Sans Serif, 8.25pt.

ForeColor	Selects the forecolor for the control.

Default: ControlText

GraphicsEnabled	Determines whether vision graphics are displayed after a sequence is run.  This property can be set "on the fly" so that graphics can be turned on/off while sequences are being run.

Default: 0 - False

Height	Specifies the height of the control.

Default:

Left	Specifies the left location of the control.

Default:

Name	Used to assign a unique name to the VideoBox control.

Default: VideoBoxXX

TabIndex	Sets the tab index for the control

Default: 0

ToolTipText	Sets the text for the tooltip.

Default:

Top	Sets the location of the top of the VideoBox control.

Default:

VideoEnabled	Determines whether video is displayed on the VideoBox.  This property can be set "on the fly" so that video can be turned on/off during program execution.

Default: 0- False

Visible	Sets if the control is Visible or not.

Default: 1 - True

Width	Sets the width of the VideoBox control.

Default:

Event	Description

Click	Executes when the user clicks the control with the mouse.

DblClick	Executes when the user double clicks the control with the mouse.
